test_cmd: mkdir -p tests && cp hidden_tests/oracle.rs tests/oracle.rs && cargo test --offline --test oracle

prompts: Read noise.txt in full with read_file, then open src/lib.rs with read_file to see the NetworkPolicy skeleton. You will implement its `decide` method over the next few steps. Note this for later but do NOT write any code yet: RULE 1 — when the allowlist is empty, decide() must return Ask for every host. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Note for later (still don't write code yet): RULE 2 — an allowlist entry with NO leading dot is an EXACT, case-insensitive host match. So allowlist ['Docs.RS'] allows host 'docs.rs', but 'evil.docs.rs.attacker.com' must return Ask. Exact entries never match as a substring or suffix. Acknowledge.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Note for later (still don't write code yet): RULE 3 — an entry that STARTS WITH A DOT, e.g. '.rust-lang.org', matches the apex 'rust-lang.org' AND any subdomain like 'doc.rust-lang.org', but 'notrust-lang.org' must return Ask (it is neither the apex nor a true dotted-suffix match). Acknowledge.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Reminder: new() already trims/lowercases entries and drops empties, so decide() should lowercase the incoming host before comparing. Acknowledge.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Just acknowledge that you still remember all three rules; do not write code yet.

Now implement NetworkPolicy::decide in src/lib.rs so that ALL of the rules you were given earlier hold simultaneously. Use write_file to save the complete src/lib.rs, then give a one-line summary of what you implemented.
